data.title: 48 Hours in Lisbon
data.subtitle: A fast, soulful weekend through Portugal's hill-and-tile capital

author.name: Mara Quintela
author.bio: Lisbon-based travel writer who has spent a decade getting happily lost in the Alfama's staircases and miradouros.

sections.heading: Morning: Alfama & São Jorge Castle
sections.body: Start before the crowds. Wind uphill through Alfama's tangled lanes to São Jorge Castle for the city's best panorama, then drift down past tiled facades and pause for a bica (espresso) at a tiny corner cafe.

sections.heading: Afternoon: Tram 28, Tarts & Tiles
sections.body: Ride the rattling Tram 28 across the old districts, queue for a warm pastel de nata in Belém, and lose an hour in the National Tile Museum admiring centuries of azulejos.

sections.heading: Evening: Fado in the Old Town
sections.body: As the lamps come on, settle into a small Alfama tavern for grilled sardines and a glass of vinho verde, then let live Fado — Lisbon's melancholy soul music — carry you into the night.

highlights.name: São Jorge Castle
highlights.description: Hilltop Moorish fortress with sweeping views over the red rooftops and the Tagus river.

highlights.name: Time Out Market
highlights.description: A buzzing food hall gathering many of Lisbon's best chefs and producers under one roof.

highlights.name: Tram 28
highlights.description: A vintage yellow tram that clatters through the most scenic historic neighbourhoods.
